column3: Description

column2: Merchant onboarding & KYC verification
column3: Enable B2B merchants to register, upload KYC documents, and pass verification before accepting payments. Demo epic for AgentOS pipeline — move to AI Worker to queue all child stories and subtasks.

column2: Checkout, payments & settlement
column3: Checkout idempotency, signed webhooks, and finance CSV exports. Contains intentional bugs in horizon-commerce-platform for the engineering agent to fix.

column2: Guided onboarding wizard with save-and-resume
column3:
*User story*
As a merchant admin, I want a 4-step onboarding wizard that saves my progress so that I can complete KYC over multiple sessions without data loss.

*Background*
Horizon Commerce onboarding drops off at document upload. The demo repo has partial draft support in onboardingService.ts but no step model.

*Acceptance criteria*
1. Given a new merchant, when onboarding starts, then 4 steps are returned (business info, documents, review, confirmation).
2. Given I save step 2, when I return within 7 days, then my draft restores at step 2.
3. Given a document URL over 10MB, when I save, then validation rejects before persist.

*Technical notes*
- Draft TTL from config.onboardingDraftTtlDays
- Audit every draft save via recordAudit

*Codebase*
- demo/horizon-commerce-platform/src/modules/merchant/onboardingService.ts
- demo/horizon-commerce-platform/src/modules/merchant/wizardSteps.ts (stub — needs 4 steps)

column2: Implement onboarding draft persistence API
column3:
*Goal*
Persist merchant onboarding drafts with step index and document list.

*Target files*
- src/modules/merchant/onboardingService.ts
- src/lib/audit.ts

*Implementation steps*
1. Ensure saveDraft stores step (1-4), documents[], updatedAt
2. Ensure loadDraft respects onboardingDraftTtlDays and clears expired drafts
3. Record audit entry onboarding.draft_saved on each save

*Definition of done*
- Unit-level manual test: save then load returns same step
- Expired draft returns null after TTL

column2: Add 4-step wizard step definitions
column3:
*Goal*
Expose getWizardSteps() returning four ordered steps for the merchant portal.

*Target files*
- src/modules/merchant/wizardSteps.ts
- src/index.ts

*Implementation steps*
1. Define WizardStep type with id, title, description
2. Return steps: business_info, documents, review, confirmation
3. Export from src/index.ts

*Definition of done*
- getWizardSteps().length === 4
- Step ids are stable strings for UI routing

column2: Fix document size validation on onboarding upload
column3:
*Goal*
Validate uploaded document byte size, not URL string length.

*Target files*
- src/modules/merchant/onboardingService.ts
- src/modules/merchant/kycValidator.ts

*Implementation steps*
1. Change saveDraft to accept documentSizesBytes: number[] parallel to documents
2. Reject any file over config.maxUploadBytes with KycValidationError
3. Add validateDocumentSizes helper in kycValidator.ts

*Definition of done*
- 10MB+ file rejected with clear error
- Valid sizes persist normally

column2: Role-based access control for merchant team
column3:
*User story*
As a org owner, I want to assign viewer, editor, billing, and owner roles so that my team has least-privilege access to refunds and API keys.

*Background*
assignRole exists but there is no central permission guard. Support and finance roles need different capabilities.

*Acceptance criteria*
1. Given Viewer role, when user attempts refund, then action is denied.
2. Given Editor role, when user creates API key, then action is denied.
3. Given role change, when saved, then audit log records actor and target.

*Codebase*
- src/modules/merchant/onboardingService.ts
- src/modules/merchant/permissionGuard.ts (stub)

column2: Extend assignRole with invite metadata
column3:
*Goal*
Track who assigned each role and when for audit compliance.

*Target files*
- src/modules/merchant/onboardingService.ts
- src/lib/audit.ts

*Implementation steps*
1. Add assignRoleBy(actorId, userId, role) that records actor in audit
2. Keep assignRole as wrapper for backward compatibility

*Definition of done*
- Audit entry includes actor, target, role

column2: Implement permissionGuard for refund and API key actions
column3:
*Goal*
Central guard used by refundService and API routes.

*Target files*
- src/modules/merchant/permissionGuard.ts
- src/modules/orders/refundService.ts

*Implementation steps*
1. Implement assertCan(userId, action) for refund, api_key_create, view_pii
2. Wire refundService to call assertCan before processing
3. Viewer and billing roles cannot refund

*Definition of done*
- Unauthorized refund throws with clear message

column2: Presigned S3 URLs for KYC document upload
column3:
*User story*
As a merchant admin, I want presigned upload URLs for KYC documents so that large files upload directly to S3 without hitting API body limits.

*Background*
documentUpload.ts is a stub. Production uses S3; demo uses fake presign URLs.

*Acceptance criteria*
1. Given merchantId and filename, when presign requested, then URL expires in 15 minutes.
2. Given invalid content type, when presign requested, then request is rejected.

*Codebase*
- src/modules/merchant/documentUpload.ts (stub)

column2: Implement createPresignedUploadUrl in documentUpload.ts
column3:
*Goal*
Return demo-safe presigned URL structure for KYC uploads.

*Target files*
- src/modules/merchant/documentUpload.ts

*Implementation steps*
1. Add createPresignedUploadUrl(merchantId, filename, contentType, sizeBytes)
2. Reject sizeBytes > config.maxUploadBytes
3. Return { url, expiresAt, fields } demo object

*Definition of done*
- Oversized file rejected
- Valid request returns url and expiresAt

column2: Fix duplicate charge on double-click Pay
column3:
*User story*
As a shopper, I want checkout to charge my card only once so that I am not double-billed when I double-click Pay.

*Background*
KNOWN BUG in checkoutService.ts — idempotency was removed for demo. processCheckout creates a new chargeId on every call.

*Acceptance criteria*
1. Given same idempotencyKey, when checkout called twice, then second call returns same charge or rejects duplicate.
2. Given different keys, when checkout called, then separate charges are created.

*Codebase*
- src/modules/payments/checkoutService.ts
- apps/merchant-portal/src/checkoutButton.ts (stub debounce)

column2: Restore idempotency key handling in processCheckout
column3:
*Goal*
Prevent duplicate charges for the same idempotencyKey.

*Target files*
- src/modules/payments/checkoutService.ts
- src/lib/errors.ts
- test/checkout.test.js

*Implementation steps*
1. Track processed idempotency keys in memory Set
2. Throw PaymentDuplicateError on duplicate key
3. Return stable chargeId for first successful charge
4. Update test to assert duplicate is rejected

*Definition of done*
- Duplicate key throws PaymentDuplicateError
- Tests pass

column2: Add Pay button debounce in merchant portal
column3:
*Goal*
Disable Pay button for 2s after first click to reduce duplicate submits.

*Target files*
- apps/merchant-portal/src/checkoutButton.ts

*Implementation steps*
1. Implement createCheckoutButtonHandler(onPay) returning debounced handler
2. Use 2000ms window
3. Export for portal integration

*Definition of done*
- Rapid double invoke only calls onPay once within 2s

column2: Fix webhook signature for payloads over 64KB
column3:
*User story*
As a integration engineer, I want webhook signatures to validate on large order payloads so that ERP sync works for large B2B orders.

*Background*
BUG: verifySignature truncates body at 64KB before HMAC. Large orders fail validation despite correct secret.

*Acceptance criteria*
1. Given 200-line-item payload, when signature verified with SDK, then validation passes.
2. Given tampered payload, when verified, then validation fails.

*Codebase*
- src/modules/payments/webhookDispatcher.ts

column2: Sign and verify full webhook body regardless of size
column3:
*Goal*
Remove 64KB truncation from verifySignature and signPayload usage.

*Target files*
- src/modules/payments/webhookDispatcher.ts

*Implementation steps*
1. Remove body.slice(0, 65536) truncation
2. Use full body string for HMAC in signPayload and verifySignature
3. Add comment referencing HC-202

*Definition of done*
- Payloads >64KB validate correctly with matching secret

column2: Settlement CSV export with date range filter
column3:
*User story*
As a finance analyst, I want CSV export of settlements filtered by date range so that month-end close does not require manual copy-paste.

*Background*
toSettlementCsv exists but lacks date filtering. exportSettlementsByDate is stubbed.

*Acceptance criteria*
1. Given start and end date, when export runs, then only rows in range are included.
2. Given empty range, when export runs, then CSV has headers only.

*Codebase*
- src/modules/payments/settlementReporter.ts
- src/modules/analytics/exportService.ts

column2: Implement exportSettlementsByDate in settlementReporter
column3:
*Goal*
Filter settlement rows by settlementDate inclusive range and return CSV.

*Target files*
- src/modules/payments/settlementReporter.ts

*Implementation steps*
1. Add exportSettlementsByDate(rows, startDate, endDate)
2. Parse settlementDate as ISO date strings
3. Reuse toSettlementCsv for output

*Definition of done*
- In-range rows exported
- Out-of-range rows excluded
